description: US-14 Configure the Business Rules — Policy values (quota N, portions, prices per head, week anchor) as settings data that takes effect the moment it is saved, plus the screen to edit them. Must be built first: registration needs N and the counter needs the week anchor.

userStories.title: Settings value objects and in-force resolution
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pure module that resolves the policy values in force at a given point in time, so every other rule reads configuration through one seam. Source: tasks/prd-us-14-configure-business-rules.md §US-14.1
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/policy/settings.ts defines the settings shape: quotaN, portionsPerGrownUp, portionsPerChild, weekAnchor {isoWeek, colour}, distributionWeekday (ISO 1-7), pricePerGrownUp and pricePerChild as integer cents
resolveSettingsAt(versions, date) returns the version with the greatest recordedAt that is <= date; of two versions recorded at the same instant the later one wins
A date before the earliest version throws a typed NoSettingsInForce error, never a partial object
priceFor(settings, grownUps, children) returns grownUps * pricePerGrownUp + children * pricePerChild — every household size is priceable
Construction validates quotaN >= 1, portion values >= 0, both prices non-negative integer cents
Tests cover: the exact instant a version was recorded, a date between versions, a date before all versions, two versions sharing an instant, a household priced per head
Module contains no I/O, no new Date() and no imports from Next.js, React or Prisma
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: SettingsRepository port with readCurrentSettings and updateSettings use cases
userStories.description: As a developer, I need use cases that read the settings in force and append a new version that applies at once, so the UI never touches persistence directly. Source: tasks/prd-us-14-configure-business-rules.md §US-14.2
acceptanceCriteria: SettingsRepository port added to src/application/ports.ts with listVersions() and append(version)
readCurrentSettings(deps) resolves versions against deps.clock.now()
updateSettings(deps, input) appends a new version stamped with deps.clock.now() and never mutates an existing one — there is no effective-from input and the saved values are in force at once
updateSettings rejects with a typed QuotaBelowActiveCustomers error carrying both numbers when the new quotaN is below the current active customer count
updateSettings writes an audit entry recording what changed, when and why, with no actor field
The reason is optional for a settings edit — the changed fields already say what happened — and is stored as an empty why; block (US-08) and archive (US-10) still require one
Tested against hand-written fakes and a fake clock
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: SettingsVersion schema, seed and Prisma repository
userStories.description: As a developer, I need settings versions stored in SQLite and seeded with provisional defaults, so a fresh install boots into a working app. Source: tasks/prd-us-14-configure-business-rules.md §US-14.3
acceptanceCriteria: Prisma model SettingsVersion (id, recordedAt, scalar policy fields, pricePerGrownUpCents, pricePerChildCents)
recordedAt is indexed but not unique — it is machine-stamped, so a same-millisecond collision is a concurrency accident, not a business error
All money columns are Int cents — no Float or Decimal anywhere in schema.prisma

A seed routine inserts the provisional version (quotaN 240, 2 portions per grown-up, 1 per child, price 200c per grown-up + 100c per child, anchor 2026-W02 = RED, weekday Thursday) only when no version exists; running it twice is a no-op
PrismaSettingsRepository implements the port and is integration-tested against a throwaway SQLite file
The placeholder SchemaMarker model is removed
Migration committed under prisma/migrations/
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Settings screen at /einstellungen
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to edit the quota, portions, prices and week-cycle settings in the app, so FD can adapt without calling a developer. Source: tasks/prd-us-14-configure-business-rules.md §US-14.4
acceptanceCriteria: Route /einstellungen shows the currently-in-force values, each labelled in German
A Zod schema validates the form; euro input (e.g. 2,50) is converted to whole cents before leaving the adapter
The two prices are edited as euro fields, one per grown-up and one per child
Saving takes effect immediately — the screen has no effective-from field — and offers an optional reason field labelled as optional
Lowering quotaN below the active customer count shows a German error naming both numbers and saves nothing
Previous versions are listed read-only with the date each was recorded
All user-facing strings come from src/i18n/de.ts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: E2E: edit a price and see it applied
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Playwright spec proving the settings round-trip works against the built app. Source: tasks/prd-us-14-configure-business-rules.md §US-14.5
acceptanceCriteria: Spec opens /einstellungen, changes the price per grown-up, saves, reloads and asserts the new value is displayed and listed in the history
Spec asserts the quota-too-low path shows an error and leaves the stored value unchanged
npm run test:e2e passes
Typecheck passes
